title: Boo It And Use It: What Gen Z Gets Right About AI
snippet: Somewhere between Pomp and Circumstance and the president's closing remarks, more than one commencement speaker this year mentioned artificial intelligence — and met a barrage of boos. To be clear: I am not here to celebrate rudeness... The paradox is real but Gen Z doesn't need us to resolve it. They're already straddling both sides: using the tools, doubting the hype, making their own calculatio
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — Ann Kirschner is listed as the sole author of this Forbes Contributor article on AI and Gen Z.

title: LinkedIn post sharing Forbes 'Boo It And Use It' AI article with original commentary
snippet: I have been thinking about those boos at graduation. And the paradox: no demographic uses AI more than Gen Z. It's their productivity tool, their brainstorming partner, their 2 a.m. confessor. The booers and the power users are the same people. Meanwhile, higher education is busy updating the honor code.

title: LinkedIn post on 'cognitive surrender' and AI's effect on human reasoning
snippet: 'Cognitive surrender' Now let's use our human brains and hearts to determine when friction is useful, and when it is useless... We need educators who will teach how and when technology integrates with learning. Which leads me to the conclusion I've been saying now for a while. Universities have a key role to play in defining the future of work, learning, and community.

title: Redesigning Education for Critical Thinking and Communication — AI reveals the need for fundamental redesign
